blocks:
Ayahs

الٓر ۚ تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَقُرْءَانٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the signs of the Book and a manifest Quran. (1)
رُّبَمَا يَوَدُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ مُسْلِمِينَ (۲)
Much will the faithless wish that they had been Muslims. (2)

Key Points

| In Tafsir Tabari and Majma' al-Bayan, under Ayah 2, a hadith is mentioned that on the Day of Resurrection, the disbelievers will say to the Muslims who are afflicted by Hell: "You, too, became inhabitants of the Fire like us, and Islam did not save you." However, the sinful Muslims will be saved after a period of punishment, while the disbelievers will remain in the Fire of Hell forever, and they will say: "If only we had been Muslims." We read in history: The Caesar of Rome, upon seeing the letter of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, intended to embrace faith, but he said, "My life is in danger, and I will lose my rule."(1)

1) Makatib al-Rasul, Vol. 1, p. 112.

Messages

1- Although the Qur'an holds a very high status, tilka (تلك), access to it is easy at the same time, because it is both written, Kitab (كتاب), and readable, Qur'an (قرآن).

2- The contents of the Qur'an are clear and delineate the boundary between truth and falsehood. Mubin (مبین).

3- Future glory belongs to Islam, and regret will be for the disbelievers. Yawaddu alladhīna kafarū (یودّ الّذین كفروا). (Those who mock Islam today will regret it in the future, and there are perhaps many disbelievers who wish for Islam but are trapped by tyrants or their corrupt environment.)

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Hijr

| This Surah has ninety-nine verses and, according to the famous saying, is the fifty-second Surah revealed to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in Mecca before the Hijra (migration). "Al-Hijr" is the name of a city where the people of Prophet Salih lived, and the name of this Surah is taken from its eightieth verse, which is about the people of Salih: كذّبت اصحاب الحجر المرسلین (The dwellers of Al-Hijr denied the Messengers). Most of the contents of this Surah concern the accusations and mockery of the disbelievers regarding the Qur'an and the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, and the command to be patient and forgiving towards them. It serves as a form of comfort and consolation for the beloved Prophet against the pressure of the opponents' attacks, following the deaths of Lady Khadijah and Lady Abu Talib علیهما السلام.

Ayah

ذَرْهُمْ يَأْكُلُوا۟ وَيَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ وَيُلْهِهِمُ ٱلْأَمَلُ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (۳)
Leave them to eat and enjoy and to be diverted by longings. Soon they will know. (3)

Key Points

The issue of leaving the disbelievers to their own devices, which is derived from the phrase "ذرهم" (leave them), is due to their own rebellion (or obstinacy). Otherwise, Allah initially sent the Prophet for the guidance of all humanity and has not abandoned anyone to their own state. This matter has been raised repeatedly in the Quran. For example: «و نذرهم فى طغیانهم یعمهون»(2), (We leave them so that they wander blindly in their transgression), and in another place He says: «و یمُدّهم فى طغیانهم»(3) (We grant them respite in their transgression). Man lives by aspiration, and if aspiration is ever taken away from man, he ceases work and effort. However, the aspiration that has been criticized in Islam relates to the following cases: 1. Prolonged aspiration, 2. Aspiration exceeding one's deeds, 3. Aspiration without action, 4. Aspiration that distracts man, 5. Aspiration for good results from evil deeds or bad people. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: I fear two things for you: one is following desires, which prevents you from the truth, and the other is prolonged aspirations, which make you heedless of the Hereafter. In Dua Kumayl, we read: Prolonged aspirations deprive man of attaining good things. «و حَبَسنى عن نفعى بُعد آمالى»

2) An'am, 110. 3) Baqarah, 15.

Messages

1- Do not spend your time on the unworthy in propagation and guidance. «ذرهم»

2- Man sometimes becomes so immersed in disbelief that even the proofs and warnings of the prophets do not awaken him. «ذرهم»

3- Aspiration is inappropriate if it makes man heedless. «و یُلههم الأمل» (But if it compels man to work and strive, it is good, because we read in the Quran: «والباقیات الصّالحات خیر عند ربّك ثواباً و خیر اَمَلاً»(4))

4- We should not consider Divine respite as a sign of His grace and mercy. «ذرهم ... فسوف یعلمون»

(Sometimes a doctor tells the patient's family: Leave the patient alone and give him whatever he wants to eat, because the situation is beyond remedy.)

4) Kahf, 46.

Ayah

وَمَآ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا وَلَهَا كِتَابٌ مَّعْلُومٌ (٤)
We did not destroy any town but that it had a known term. (4)

Messages

1- The worldly successes of those devoted to this world have an end. "ما اهلكنا من قریة"

2- If We wish, We can destroy the disbelievers immediately, but Our program and tradition (Sunnah) is to grant opportunity and delay. "لها كتاب معلوم"

3- Let us not be deceived by the divine respites. "لها كتاب معلوم"

4- Society and history also have laws, timing, and principles. "قریة الا ولها كتاب معلوم"

Ayah

مَّا تَسْبِقُ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلَهَا وَمَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ (٥)
No nation can advance its time nor can it defer it. (5)

Key Points

The appointed term (ajal) and the events that occur are of two types: definite and indefinite. The indefinite term (ajal) is changeable through supplication, charity, and good deeds, but the definite term (ajal) is not changeable.

Messages

1- Not only individuals but also nations and their history have a timeline and an appointed term (ajal). «اجلاً»

2- The survival and stability of individuals and nations are in the Hand of Allah, and man has no role in advancing or delaying the definite Divine programs. «ما تسبق ... و ما یستأخرون»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِى نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلذِّكْرُ إِنَّكَ لَمَجْنُونٌ (٦)
They said, ‘O you, to whom the Reminder has been sent down, you are indeed crazy. (6)
لَّوْ مَا تَأْتِينَا بِٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۷)
Why do you not bring us the angels should you be truthful?!’ (7)

Key Points

The word majnūn (مجنون) does not mean "senseless" or "irrational," but rather "jinn-possessed." Similarly, the word dīvāneh (دیوانه) means "demon-possessed." During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), they believed that a poet could compose poetry due to his connection with the jinn. In these two verses, contempt, mockery, accusation, and doubt are presented against the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, expressed by the disbelievers, using various forms of emphasis. The phrase yā ayyuhal-ladhī (یاایهاالذّى) instead of yā ayyuhan-nabī (یاایهاالنّبى) is a form of contempt. The word dhikr (ذكر), given that the disbelievers did not believe in revelation, is a form of mockery. Majnūn (مجنون) is an accusation, and the sentence in kunta min al-ṣādiqīn (ان كنت من الصادقین) is a sign of their doubt regarding his prophethood. Furthermore, the particle inna (اِنّ) and the particle lām (لام) in the phrase innaka la-majnūn (انّك لمجنون), along with the nominal sentence structure, indicate various types of emphasis in their speech and deviant belief.

Messages

1- Attention to spirituality is considered madness by some groups. Innaka la-majnūn (انك لمجنون).

2- The disbelievers did not accept the source of the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله words, which is revelation. Therefore, instead of saying, "Allah sent down to you" (anzala Allāhu ilayka), they said, "It was sent down upon him" (nuzzila 'alayhi).

3- The disbelievers considered the descent of an angel to be the sign of the Prophet's sincerity, but this was nothing more than an excuse. For in another place in the Quran, we read: Even if We were to send down the angels to them, and the dead were to come alive and speak to them, and We were to present everything before their eyes, they still would not believe. Mā kānū li-yu'minū (ما كانوا لیؤمنوا).

Ayah

مَا نُنَزِّلُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَمَا كَانُوٓا۟ إِذًا مُّنظَرِينَ (۸)
We do not send down the angels except with due reason, and then they will not be granted any respite. (8)

Key Points

It is understood from the verses of the Quran that whenever a miracle is issued by the prophets and people ignore it, divine wrath might descend, or they might be given a respite for a period. However, whenever the type of miracle and its request comes from the people themselves—such as the camel emerging from the heart of the mountain, which they requested from Prophet Salih (عليه السلام), or the descent of heavenly food, which they requested from Prophet Isa (عليه السلام)—in such cases, if the people transgress, God's wrath is certain and immediate, and they are no longer given respite. "و ما كانوا اذاً منظرین" (and they would not then be given respite). In the previous Ayah, the disbelievers, using the phrase "لو ما تأتینا بالملائكة" (Why do you not bring us the angels?), requested the descent of angels upon themselves from the noble Prophet. This Ayah responds: the descent of angels is based on truth (Haqq), and the truth is that the angel descends upon worthy individuals. The descent of the angel upon unworthy individuals is falsehood (Batil), and We do nothing but the truth. "ما ننزل الملائكة الا بالحقّ" (We do not send down the angels except with the truth).

Messages

1- The descent of angels is not based on the whims of this person or that, but rather on the basis of truth. "وما ننزّل الملائكة الاّ بالحق"

2- Allah's granting of respite also follows a law. In cases where the type of miracle was requested by the people themselves, yet they still rebel, there is no respite involved. "كانوا اذاً منظرین"

Ayah

إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا ٱلذِّكْرَ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَـٰفِظُونَ (۹)
Indeed We have sent down the Reminder, and indeed We will preserve it. (9)

Key Points

In Ayah 6, the disbelievers were saying: It is unclear what the source of the Prophet's dhikr (remembrance/message) is, [referring to] "نزّل علیه" (it was sent down upon him). In this Ayah [Ayah 9], He says: Do not doubt; certainly, We Ourselves are the ones who send down the Dhikr (the Qur'an) upon him. And just as the disbelievers emphatically attributed madness [to the Prophet], Allah, with even greater emphasis, attributes the descent of the Qur'an and its preservation to His Holy Essence.

In this short Ayah, six types of emphasis have been used; three emphases for the descent of the Qur'an, which are embedded in the words "انّ" (Inna), "نحن" (Nahnu), and "الذكر" (al-Dhikr), and three emphases for the preservation of the Qur'an from any kind of change or alteration, which are in the words "انّ" (Inna), "له" (Lahu), and "لَ" (La), which is clear to specialists in the terminology of Arabic literature.

Based on the testimony and promise of Allah in this Ayah, no change or distortion (tahrif) has occurred in the Qur'an, just as this matter is mentioned in other verses, including Ayah 42 of Fussilat: "لایأتیه الباطل من بین یدیه ولا من خلفه" (Falsehood shall not come to it from before it or from behind it). No falsehood can find a way into the Qur'an through any means.

And if we encounter narrations (riwayat) that hint at the distortion of the Qur'an; they are either fabricated, which must be discarded, or the intent is the distortion in meaning and practice, interpretation based on personal opinion and preference (tafsir bi al-ra'y), and the sidelining of the true experts of the Qur'an, namely the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام.

In addition to Allah's promise regarding the protection of the Qur'an, Muslims, from the beginning, memorized the Qur'an and were serious about writing and preserving it. They even made the teaching of the Qur'an the dowry (mahr) for women, they recited it in their prayers, and the scribes of revelation were numerous, one of whom was Hadrat Ali علیه السلام. The Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام invited people to this very existing Qur'an.

Furthermore, the Hadith of Thaqalayn (The Two Weighty Things) from the Prophet himself, who said: I am leaving two precious things among you and departing; one is the Qur'an and the other is my Ahl al-Bayt. These two shall never separate from each other, and if you hold fast to both, you will never go astray. This is another testimony to the integrity of the Qur'an. And truly, can it be said that the Prophet's mission is permanent but His Book is subject to change?!

The favors of the Truth promised Mustafa (the Chosen One): "If you die, this system (nasq) shall not perish. I am the Preserver of your Book and your miracle; I reject anyone who adds or subtracts from the Qur'an."

Hundreds of books, articles, and research papers have been authored by great Shia scholars concerning the non-distortion of the Qur'an, and it is with great regret that the belief in distortion has been unjustly attributed to the Shia.

The Qur'an is Dhikr (remembrance), "نزّلنا الذكر" (We have sent down the Dhikr), and turning away from Dhikr and the Qur'an leads man to misery. "و من اعرض عن ذكرى فان له معیشة ضنكا" (And whoever turns away from My remembrance—indeed, for him is a restricted life) (5). The Qur'an is Dhikr, and only through the remembrance of Allah do hearts find tranquility. "الا بذكر اللَّه تطمئن القلوب" (Unquestionably, by the remembrance of Allah hearts are assured) (6).

5) Taha, 124. 6) Ra'd, 28.

Messages

  1. The Qur'an is neither from man nor from an angel. "انّا نحن" (Indeed, We).
  2. The Qur'an is preserved forever. "لحافظون" (La-Hafizun) (The active participle form signifies permanence).
  3. One of the prophecies of the Qur'an and one of the distinctions of Islam is the immunity of the Qur'an from distortion. "انا له لحافظون" (Indeed, We are its Preservers).
  4. The Qur'an is Dhikr; remembrance of Allah, remembrance of blessings, remembrance of the history of past generations, and remembrance of the Day of Judgment. "الذكر" (The Dhikr).
  5. Unjust accusations and insults must be answered firmly and in kind. The statement "نُزّل علیه الذكر" (The Dhikr was sent down upon him) must be answered with the Ayah "نحن نزلنا الذكر" (We have sent down the Dhikr).

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ فِى شِيَعِ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱٠)
Certainly We sent [apostles] before you to former communities, (10)
وَمَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۱۱)
and there did not come to them any apostle but that they used to deride him. (11)

Key Points

The term Shi' (شِیع) means a community or group that possesses a type of bond and cohesion, whether that bond is upon the path of truth, like the Ayah: «و انّ من شیعته لابراهیم» (7), or upon the path of deviation: «فرقوا دینهم و كانوا شیعا» (8). The goal of the mockery (istihza') was either to break the majesty (ubbah) of the Prophets so that truth-seeking people would not gather around them, or it was to compensate for their own weakness and inability in the face of the Prophets' logic. And the things used as a pretext for ridicule were either the simple life of the Prophets, or their deprived followers, or the breaking of superstitious traditions.

7) Saffat, 83. 8) An'am, 159.

Messages

1- In the face of the flood of mockery, individuals must be strengthened. «لقد ارسلنا من قبلك»

2- Paying attention to history and the difficulties faced by others makes the endurance of difficulties easier for a person. «ارسلنا من قبلك»

3- None of the Prophets were immune from being mocked by the disbelievers. «ما یأتیهم من رسول» (The preacher should not become disheartened by people's ridicule.)

4- Mockery has been the constant practice and method of the disbelievers against the Prophets. «كانوا به یستهزؤن» (The weapon of one who lacks logic is ridicule.)

Ayahs

كَذَٰلِكَ نَسْلُكُهُۥ فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۱۲)
That is how We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty: (12)
لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۖ وَقَدْ خَلَتْ سُنَّةُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۳)
they do not believe in it, and the precedent of the ancients has already passed. (13)
وَلَوْ فَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَابًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَظَلُّوا۟ فِيهِ يَعْرُجُونَ (۱٤)
Were We to open for them a gate of the sky, so that they could go on ascending through it, (14)
لَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصَـٰرُنَا بَلْ نَحْنُ قَوْمٌ مَّسْحُورُونَ (۱٥)
they would surely say, ‘Indeed a spell has been cast on our eyes; indeed, we are a bewitched lot.’ (15)

Key Points

We read in a few preceding verses that the disbelievers requested the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to show them or bring an angel. In Ayah 14 of this Surah, He says: Even if We were to open a door from the heaven for them, and they were to ascend to the heaven and see the angels and other things, they still would not believe, because the request to see an angel is merely an excuse. And the obstinate person would even deny his own ascension to the heaven. In Ayah 7 of Surah Al-An'am, we read: Even if We had sent down to them a Book on paper from the heaven, and they had touched it with their own hands, they would still deny it and say, "This is nothing but manifest sorcery and magic." Some commentators say that "نسلكه" (Naslukuhu) means that We have caused this denial and mockery to penetrate the hearts of the disbelievers, similar to the verse "ختم اللَّه على قلوبهم" (Khatama Allahu 'ala qulubihim), meaning they have become hard-hearted and are no longer receptive to guidance.

Messages

1- Allah's tradition (Sunnah) for guiding people is the same for everyone. «كذلك نسلكه»

2- Allah completes the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah) and conveys the truth to the hearts and depths of people's souls, so that they cannot say, "We did not understand the truth." «فى قلوب المجرمین»

3- Crime and sin distance a person from accepting guidance. «قلوب المجرمین، لایؤمنون به»

4- Merely understanding the truth is not enough to accept it, because believing requires will, humility, and submission. «نسلكه فى قلوب ... لا یؤمنون»

5- The heaven has doors which, by the will of Allah, are opened for whomever He wills. «فتحنا علیهم باباً»

6- Obstinacy prevents recognition, and the obstinate person denies even the perceptible realities. «لو فتحنا علیهم باباً... لقالوا انّما سكرت ابصارنا»

7- Obstinacy is an incurable and expanding ailment. Upon seeing any sign, the obstinate person first says, "My eyes have been bewitched," «انّما سكرت ابصارنا» but later says, "Our entire being has been bewitched." «بل نحن قوم مسحورون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ (۱٦)
Certainly We have appointed houses in the sky and adorned them for the onlookers, (16)

Key Points

The word 'Burūj' (بروج) is the plural of 'Burj' (بُرج), which originally means 'manifestation' or 'appearance.' A woman who displays her adornments is said to be 'tabarrajat al-mar'ah' (تبرجت المرئة). A burj is also used for a palace, mansion, or tall building that has a special prominence. In this verse, the celestial bodies or their stations (manāzil) are likened to burj. From the Earth's orbit around the Sun, a hypothetical circle called the 'Zodiac' (Mintaqat al-Burūj) is formed. This circle has been divided into twelve parts, and stars are located corresponding to each part. A cluster of stars is named after the shape of that corresponding section. In Persian, they are named Farvardin, Ordibehesht, Khordad, ..... and in Arabic, they are named Ḥamal (Aries), Thawr (Taurus), Jawzā' (Gemini), Saraṭān (Cancer), Asad (Leo), Sunbulah (Virgo), Mīzān (Libra), 'Aqrab (Scorpio), Qaws (Sagittarius), Jadī (Capricorn), Dalw (Aquarius), and Ḥūt (Pisces).

Messages

1- The celestial bodies and their movement in various orbits are among the signs of Monotheism (Tawḥīd) and the knowledge of Allah. «لقد جعلنا»

2- The celestial creations are also contingent (hādith) [i.e., created, not eternal]. «جعلنا»

3- Adornment and beauty are fundamental principles in creation, which Allah has observed in the creation of the heavens. «زیّناها»

4- The heaven, just like the earth, has been created for mankind. «للناظرین»

Ayahs

وَحَفِظْنَـٰهَا مِن كُلِّ شَيْطَـٰنٍ رَّجِيمٍ (۱۷)
and We have guarded them from every outcast Satan, (17)
إِلَّا مَنِ ٱسْتَرَقَ ٱلسَّمْعَ فَأَتْبَعَهُۥ شِهَابٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۸)
except someone who may eavesdrop, whereat there pursues him a manifest flame. (18)

Key Points

"Istirāq" (eavesdropping) is derived from sariqa (theft), and "Istirāq al-Sam'" means the theft of speech. In the commentaries Fi Zilal and Maraghi, we read: We do not know the essence of the Ayah—how Satan steals news and what news He steals. However, Fakhr Razi and Alusi state in their commentaries: The intent of "heaven" is this apparent sky, and the intent of the shihāb (meteor) is these celestial stones that are hurled and ignited. Although some have said: The intent of "heaven" is the realm of Malakūt (the spiritual realm), to which the devils have no access. In any case, perhaps it can be said: In the heaven of spirituality, We have placed shining figures, role models, and leaders, and We have protected the truths from the whispers of Satan. And whenever a devilish person whispers, We attack him with strong argumentation and logic, and We obliterate his innovation, syncretism, and whispers with the meteor of knowledge and the proof of the People of Remembrance (Ahl al-Dhikr).

Messages

1- The heavens are the center for the management of affairs, and the angels are the managers of affairs, and centers of management must be protected zones. «حفظناها»

2- Satan is not only Iblis. «كلّ شیطان»

3- Satan does not leave on his own; he must be expelled. «رجیم»

4- Eavesdropping and spying in evil matters is the work of Satan. «من استرق السمع»

5- A spy must be dealt with swiftly and decisively. «فاتبعه شهاب»

6- Wherever a devilish person is found, the enlighteners of society must pursue them with the meteor of knowledge. «فاتبعه شهاب مبین»

7- The response to whispers and devilish acts must be very explicit, public, and immediate. «فاتبعه شهاب مبین» (In Surah Saffat, Ayah 8, we read: «یُقذفون من كلّ جانب»—the expulsion of Satan must be from all directions, otherwise he will manifest in another form or through another path.)

Ayahs

وَٱلْأَرْضَ مَدَدْنَـٰهَا وَأَلْقَيْنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ وَأَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ مَّوْزُونٍ (۱۹)
And We spread out the earth, and cast in it firm mountains, and We grew in it every kind of balanced thing, (19)
وَجَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَعَـٰيِشَ وَمَن لَّسْتُمْ لَهُۥ بِرَٰزِقِينَ (۲٠)
and made in it [various] means of livelihood for you and for those whom you do not provide for. (20)

Key Points

The meaning of casting down the mountains (ilqā al-kūh), like casting down a doubt (ilqā al-shubhah), is to establish it. The word "rawāsī" is the plural of "rāsiya," meaning firm and steadfast. The word "ma'āyish" is the plural of "ma'īshah," meaning the means of livelihood.

Messages

1- One of Allah's blessings is the vastness and levelness of the earth. «مددنا» (Otherwise, agriculture and many other efforts would be carried out with difficulty.)

2- The vastness of the earth and the appearance of mountains and plants are not accidental. «مَدَنا القینا انبتنا»

3- Divine creations have been created based on a specific measure and law. «من كل شى موزون»

4- The earth is ready for the growth of every kind of plant. «كل شى ء»

5- The creation of the earth, mountains, and plants is for mankind. «لكم»

6- Allah is responsible for the livelihood and sustenance (rizq) of all creatures, even those whose provision you are unable to secure. «و من لستم له برازقین»

Ayah

وَإِن مِّن شَىْءٍ إِلَّا عِندَنَا خَزَآئِنُهُۥ وَمَا نُنَزِّلُهُۥٓ إِلَّا بِقَدَرٍ مَّعْلُومٍ (۲۱)
There is not a thing but that its sources are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure. (21)

Key Points

Several verses refer to the issue of measurement (or precise determination) in the creation of beings, including: «قد جَعَل اللَّه لكلّ شى ء قدرا»(9) «كل شى ء عنده بمقدار»(10) «و خلق كلّ شى ء فقدره تقدیرا»(11) In Ayah 27 of Surah Ash-Shura, we read that if Allah were to expand the provision (rizq) for His servants, they would commit transgression. For this reason, He gives to everyone only the amount He deems appropriate. The verse under discussion also refers to this meaning. Perhaps the intent behind "treasuries" (khazā’in) is the means, elements, and potential capabilities that Allah has placed within every being. The phrase «ان من شى ء الاّ عندنا خزائنه» encompasses every created thing. For example, the ability and power of sight and hearing also have a source and treasury with Allah, from which a specific amount reaches us. For instance, if He wills, we could hear the sound of an ant's footsteps on the other side of the Earth. Furthermore, modern science has discovered that every plant possesses a specific weight, such that if its elements and components are slightly increased or decreased, it ceases to be that particular plant. Descent (nuzūl) is sometimes from a higher place, like the descent of rain, and sometimes from a higher status (or realm), like the verse «و انزلنا الحدید»(12), which means the creation of iron. In this verse, we read that blessings have measure and calculation, and the amount of each is known, «بقدر معلوم». Some of the factors influencing this have been explained in verses and narrations. Human effort can be effective in determining provision (rizq); possessing good character, high expertise, broad-mindedness (sa'at sadr), good intention (husn niyyat), fervor, and the prayers of the righteous can influence that known amount.

9) At-Talaq, 3. 10) Ar-Ra'd, 8. 11) Al-Furqan, 2. 12) Al-Hadid, 25.

Messages

1- Existence has a source and origin that is under Allah's control, and only a portion of it reaches us, of which we are aware. «عندنا خزائنه»

2- Nothing in existence has been created without a specific measure. «بقدر معلوم»

3- The Divine treasuries are numerous, and the descent of His mercy is gradual. «نُنزله» (Tanzīl means gradual descent.)

4- The Divine treasuries are permanent, because whatever is with Him remains.(13) «عندنا خزائنه»

5- Do not turn to anyone other than Allah, because the source of everything you desire is with Him. «عندنا خزائنه»

6- Possession is not a justification for consumption; rather, in consumption, wisdom, capacity, and dozens of other points must be considered. «و ما ننزله الا بقدر معلوم»

7- Not all sources of existence have been discovered by humanity. «عندنا خزائنه»

13) «عنداللَّه باق» An-Nahl, 96.

Ayah

وَأَرْسَلْنَا ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ لَوَٰقِحَ فَأَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَسْقَيْنَـٰكُمُوهُ وَمَآ أَنتُمْ لَهُۥ بِخَـٰزِنِينَ (۲۲)
And We send the fertilizing winds and send down water from the sky providing it for you to drink and you are not maintainers of its resources. (22)

Key Points

Lawāqih (لواقح) is derived from liqāḥ (لقاح), meaning the fertilization of clouds through the connection of positive and negative clouds, or it means the fertilization of plants through the transfer of pollen. In Ayah 41 of Surah Adh-Dhāriyāt, we read that sometimes the wind is barren (‘aqīm) and useless, and sometimes it becomes the source of blessings. Just as we read in Ayah 43 of Surah An-Nūr: «اَلَم تَرَ اَنّ اللَّه یُزجى سحاباً ثمّ یؤلف بینه ثمّ یجعله رُكاماً فترى الودق یخرج من خلاله» (Do you not see that Allah drives the scattered clouds, then joins them together, then makes them into a mass, and you see the rain emerge from within them?) Man cannot retain water for a long period, neither in the clouds, nor on the ground (because it sinks), nor in rocky valleys (because it evaporates).

Messages

1- He is the Director and Planner of existence. «أرسلنا، أنزلنا، اسقینا»

2- Man is incapable of retaining even the most essential needs. «و ما أنتم بخازنین»

3- The blessing of water is distinct from the blessing of being quenched. «فَاَسقیناكموه» (The fact that man drinks water and is quenched is itself a blessing in addition to the blessing of water, because it was possible for a thirsty person to drink water but not be quenched.)

Ayahs

وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ نُحْىِۦ وَنُمِيتُ وَنَحْنُ ٱلْوَٰرِثُونَ (۲۳)
Indeed it is We who give life and bring death and We are the inheritors. (23)
وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا ٱلْمُسْتَقْدِمِينَ مِنكُمْ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا ٱلْمُسْتَـْٔخِرِينَ (۲٤)
Certainly We know the predecessors among you and certainly We know the successors, (24)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٥)
and indeed it is your Lord who will resurrect them. Indeed, He is all-wise, all-knowing. (25)

Key Points

Commentators have mentioned several interpretations (مصادیق) for «مستقدمین» and «مستأخرین», including: 1. Those who have passed away and those who remain. 2. Those who rush forward (to good deeds) and those who love the battlefield (jihad), and others. 3. Those who attend the first row of congregational prayer and those who attend the last row in order to cast evil glances at the women present in the congregation, regarding whom the Ayah says, "We know." Conversely, some had reached such a level of goodness that they put their homes up for sale in order to acquire a residence near the mosque and reach the first row, regarding whom the Ayah says, "We also know these individuals."

Messages

1- Death and life are solely in His hands. Everyone is destined to depart, so it is fitting that we leave behind a good inheritance through our deeds for the true Inheritor. «نحن الوارثون»

2- Time has no effect on Allah's knowledge. His knowledge of the past, present, and future is uniform. «علمنا المستقدمین... علمنا المستأخرین»

3- Establishing the Resurrection, and giving reward and punishment, are among the functions (affairs) of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّك هو یحشر»

4- The reason for the Resurrection is Allah's Wisdom. (If particles of dust become food, and food becomes sperm, and sperm becomes a human, and then through death, it turns back into dust, and there is no reckoning or accountability involved, this action is not wise.) «انّه حكیم»

5- In the Resurrection, the old and the new will be gathered together. «یحشرهم» And Allah has complete knowledge of the performance, effects, and intentions of every individual. «حكیم علیم»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِن صَلْصَـٰلٍ مِّنْ حَمَإٍ مَّسْنُونٍ (۲٦)
Certainly We created man out of a dry clay [drawn] from an aging mud, (26)
وَٱلْجَآنَّ خَلَقْنَـٰهُ مِن قَبْلُ مِن نَّارِ ٱلسَّمُومِ (۲۷)
and We created the jinn earlier, out of a piercing fire. (27)

Key Points

Ṣalṣāl (صلصال) refers to dry, unbaked clay which, when struck, produces a ringing sound. (14) Ḥama’ (حَمَأ) refers to dark-colored clay, and masnūn (مسنون) means altered or changed.

Samūm (سموم) refers to a scorching wind that affects the depth of a person's soul, like poison (samm).

This verse either refers to the creation of the first human, meaning Prophet Adam, "خلقتنى من نار و خلقته من طین" (15) or it refers to the process of creation for all humans, where earthly materials transform into a human being through food and sperm (nutfa), just as we read in Surah Al-Kahf, Ayah 37, that man first comes from dust, then from sperm, and then develops into a man. "اكفرت بالذّى خلقك من تراب ثمّ من نطفة ثمّ سواك رجلاً" (16)

In the culture of the Quran, the Jinn (جنّ) are accountable beings who have been addressed by Allah, "یا معشر الجن و الانس" (O company of Jinn and men), and they understand the Quran. In the first verse of Surah Al-Jinn, we read: "استمع نفر من الجنّ" (A group of Jinn listened to it).

The Jinn, like humans, possess desires (shahwah). For instance, concerning the women of Paradise, we read that they are virgins; neither man nor Jinn has touched them. "لم یطمثهن انسٌ قبلهم و لاجان" (17) And according to this verse, their creation was from fire and occurred before that of man. Just as Iblis is from the Jinn, "كان من الجنّ" (He was of the Jinn), and like other disbelievers among the Jinn, he will go to Hell. "لَاَملاَن جهنّم من الجنّة والنّاس اجمعین" (18)

14) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

15) Ṣād, 76.

16) Al-Kahf, 37.

17) Ar-Raḥmān, 74.

18) Hūd, 119.

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى خَـٰلِقٌۢ بَشَرًا مِّن صَلْصَـٰلٍ مِّنْ حَمَإٍ مَّسْنُونٍ (۲۸)
When your Lord said to the angels, ‘Indeed I am going to create a human out of a dry clay [drawn] from an aging mud. (28)

Key Points

Bishar (بشر) refers to the outer appearance of the skin.

Messages

1- The creation of the angels preceded that of mankind. (Because Allah discussed the matter with the angels before the creation of man.) «و اذ قال ربّك للملائكة»

2- The creation of man is from earth and material things, and thus his instinctive inclination is also towards material things. «خالق من بشر من صلصال...»

Ayahs

فَإِذَا سَوَّيْتُهُۥ وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِى فَقَعُوا۟ لَهُۥ سَـٰجِدِينَ (۲۹)
So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My spirit, then fall down in prostration before him.’ (29)
فَسَجَدَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ كُلُّهُمْ أَجْمَعُونَ (۳٠)
Thereat the angels prostrated, all of them together, (30)
إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ مَعَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ (۳۱)
but not Iblis: he refused to be among those who prostrated. (31)

Key Points

The meaning of Allah breathing His Spirit into man is not merely having life and breathing, because animals also breathe. Rather, the intention is the bestowal of attributes such as creativity, will, and knowledge upon man by God. And attributing the spirit (Ruh) to Allah is for the honor of the spirit, similar to Bayt Allah (House of Allah) and Shahr Allah (Month of Allah). The prostration of the angels before Adam was not a ceremonial prostration, but rather meant their humility before Adam and his progeny. Meaning, the angels are also in the service of mankind and subservient to him (19).

(19) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The creation of man is complete and balanced. «سوّیته»

2- The prostration of the angels was due to the breathing of Allah's Spirit. «نفخت فیه من روحى فقعوا»

The body of man is honored by the soul of humanity Not merely beautiful clothing is the sign of humanity

3- Man is a two-dimensional being, and he is complete in both dimensions. In the material dimension, «سوّیته» (I fashioned him); in the spiritual dimension, «نفخت فیه من روحى» (I breathed into him of My Spirit).

4- Man is the manifestation of the effulgence of some of Allah's attributes. «من روحى» (of My Spirit)

5- Spirituality requires a body and a material vehicle. «سوّیته و نفخت فیه...»

6- Those who are unwilling to be in the ranks of the prostrators possess an Iblis-like spirit. «الاّ ابلیس اَبى ...» (Except Iblis, he refused...)

7- Although prostration (sujud) and bowing (ruku') are valuable, they possess a more complete value if performed alongside the bowing and prostration of other worshippers (20). «مع السّاجدین» (with those who prostrate)

(20) «واركعوا مع الراكعین» (And bow down with those who bow down), Surah Al-Baqarah, 43.

Ayahs

قَالَ يَـٰٓإِبْلِيسُ مَا لَكَ أَلَّا تَكُونَ مَعَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ (۳۲)
He said, ‘O Iblis! What kept you from being among those who have prostrated?’ (32)
قَالَ لَمْ أَكُن لِّأَسْجُدَ لِبَشَرٍ خَلَقْتَهُۥ مِن صَلْصَـٰلٍ مِّنْ حَمَإٍ مَّسْنُونٍ (۳۳)
Said he, ‘I will not prostrate before a human whom You have created out of a dry clay [drawn] from an aging mud.’ (33)
قَالَ فَٱخْرُجْ مِنْهَا فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٌ (۳٤)
He said, ‘Begone hence, for you are indeed an outcast, (34)
وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكَ ٱللَّعْنَةَ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (۳٥)
and indeed the curse shall lie on you until the Day of Retribution.’ (35)

Messages

1- In judgment, do not rely solely on your own knowledge; rather, take confession from the criminal and, through questioning and answering, clarify the source of the crime and the criminal's mindset for everyone. «یا ابلیس ما لك»

2- If the spirit of arrogance takes root in a person, the environment and other factors will be ineffective in guiding him. (Iblis was among the angels and in a celestial environment, but because of obstinacy and arrogance, he became as he did.) «الا یكون مع السّاجدین»

3- Racial superiority is an Iblisian (Satanic) idea. «خلقته من صلصال...»

4- Worse than refusing to prostrate is pride and arrogance in the face of God's command. «لم اكن لِاَسجد»

5- Worse than committing a sin is justifying the sin. «خلقته من صلصال» (Satan justified his sin.)

6- When pride and arrogance arrive, one fails to see the divine light and spirit in man. Satan saw the earthly body of man and refused to prostrate, while Allah's command to prostrate was because of man's divine spirit. «خلقته من صلصال»

7- Divine commands and orders do not necessitate compulsion. «لم اكن لاسجد»

8- Arrogance does not bring greatness; rather, it causes humiliation. «فاخرج» It causes the destruction and erasure of previous acts of worship. «فانك رجیم»

9- Ijtihad (independent reasoning) and expressing opinions in the face of a clear divine command are not permissible. In the face of the command: «فقعوا له ساجدین», there is no room for the statement «خلقته من صلصال».

10- A single moment of disobedience and arrogance led to eternal expulsion. «الى یوم الدین»

11- The arrogant person is perpetually subject to divine curse. «علیك الّعنة الى یوم الدین»

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ فَأَنظِرْنِىٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ (۳٦)
He said, ‘My Lord! Respite me till the day they will be resurrected.’ (36)
قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنظَرِينَ (۳۷)
Said He, ‘You are indeed among the reprieved (37)
إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْوَقْتِ ٱلْمَعْلُومِ (۳۸)
until the day of the known time.’ (38)

Key Points

When Satan realized he was cursed until the Day of Resurrection, he asked Allah to grant him respite until that day, but he did not say why he wanted the respite. Since it is Allah's tradition (Sunnah) to grant respite, he was given respite, but not until the Day of Resurrection as he requested, rather until a day whose time is known to God, and according to some commentators, until the day humanity exists and religious obligation (taklif) remains.

Messages

1- It is Allah's tradition (Sunnah) to grant respite to sinners. «انك من المنظرین»

2- The prayer and request of sinners are also answered if it is expedient (in Allah's wisdom). Satan said: «فانظرنى» The response came: «انك من المنظرین»

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ بِمَآ أَغْوَيْتَنِى لَأُزَيِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۳۹)
He said, ‘My Lord! As You have consigned me to perversity, I will surely glamorize [evil] for them on the earth, and I will surely pervert them, all (39)
إِلَّا عِبَادَكَ مِنْهُمُ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (٤٠)
except Your dedicated servants among them.’ (40)

Key Points

Allah does not lead anyone astray, but if someone chooses the path of deviation by his own will, Allah abandons him to his own state. And this abandonment is the greatest divine punishment and wrath. Iblis (Satan) also, because he showed arrogance and obstinacy by his own will, Allah abandoned him to his own state. The meaning intended by the phrase «اغویتنى» (You led me astray) is exactly this; meaning: now that, because of my obstinacy, You have removed me from the orbit of Your grace and left me to my own state, I will do such and such. Iblis knows that a group of people will be among the chosen ones of Allah (meaning he has accepted prophethood and Imamate as well, just as it is clear from the phrase «ربّ بما اغویتنى» (My Lord, because You have led me astray) that he has accepted God, and it is clear from his prayer, 'Grant me respite until the Day of Resurrection,' that he also accepted the Resurrection (Ma'ad). Therefore, Iblis's fundamental beliefs were correct, but his flaw was arrogance and the lack of a spirit of submission and servitude ('ubudiyyah). Thus, knowledge and faith alone are not sufficient; action, submission, and servitude are necessary.

Messages

1- Iblis and those who possess the characteristics of Iblis attribute their own fault to God. «اغویتنى»

2- Satan's tool for deviation is making ugliness appear beautiful. «لَاُزیننّ»

3- The pure and the sincere (Mukhlasin) are safe from Satan's trap. «المخلصین» (Of course, merely having sincerity (Ikhlas) is not enough; Allah's grace is necessary. Because the word «مخلَص» (Mukhlas) means the divinely chosen one.)

Ayahs

قَالَ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ عَلَىَّ مُسْتَقِيمٌ (٤۱)
He said, ‘This is the path [leading] straight to Me. (41)
إِنَّ عِبَادِى لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَـٰنٌ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْغَاوِينَ (٤۲)
Indeed, as for My servants you do not have any authority over them, except the perverse who follow you, (42)

Key Points

| In the previous Ayah, Iblis said: I will mislead everyone except the chosen and sincere ones. Allah says in this Ayah: That you cannot overcome My chosen servants is My straight path and My tradition (Sunnah). Meaning, My tradition is that I Myself guarantee their protection, not that you simply leave them alone, but rather that you cannot affect them. Question: If Iblis cannot overcome the chosen servants, then what was the temptation of Adam regarding eating from the forbidden tree? Answer: Iblis covets them and tempts them, and sometimes compels them to commit a tark al-awla (leaving the preferable action), but he does not mislead or deviate them.

Messages

1- Satan has no dominion over those who are servants of Allah and submit to Him. «عبادى لیس لك علیهم سلطان»

2- Man follows Iblis by his own choice. «اتبعك»

3- If, through worship and piety, we enter the sphere of Allah's servants, we become protected (insured). «عبادى لیس لك...»

4- The work of Satan is temptation, not dominion. «لیس لك علیهم سلطان» (Also on the Day of Judgment, Iblis will say in response to the people's objection: I only invited you, but I had no dominion or compulsion over you.)

Ayahs

وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمَوْعِدُهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٤۳)
and indeed hell is the tryst of them all. (43)
لَهَا سَبْعَةُ أَبْوَٰبٍ لِّكُلِّ بَابٍ مِّنْهُمْ جُزْءٌ مَّقْسُومٌ (٤٤)
It has seven gates, and to each gate belongs a separate portion of them.’ (44)

Key Points

The seven gates of Hell (Dūzakh) perhaps refer to their great number, meaning that many factors cause a person to become deserving of Hellfire. This is similar to Ayah 27 of Luqmān, which states: If the trees were pens and seven seas became ink, «والبحر یمده سبعة ابحر» (and the sea is supplied by seven seas) they could not write the Words of Allah. This is an allusion to the fact that the Divine creations are so numerous that they cannot be counted or calculated.

Messages

1- Both Paradise and Hell have paths (or ways). «لها سبعة ابواب»

(We read in Nahj al-Balāghah that the Mujāhidūn (striving ones) have a specific gate for Paradise.)

2- Hell, like Paradise, has levels and ranks, and everyone is punished according to his crime. «لكل باب منهم جزء مقسوم»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (٤٥)
Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and springs. (45)
ٱدْخُلُوهَا بِسَلَـٰمٍ ءَامِنِينَ (٤٦)
‘‘Enter it in peace and safety!’’ (46)
وَنَزَعْنَا مَا فِى صُدُورِهِم مِّنْ غِلٍّ إِخْوَٰنًا عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مُّتَقَـٰبِلِينَ (٤۷)
We will remove whatever rancour there is in their breasts; [intimate like] brothers, [they will be reclining] on couches, facing one another. (47)
لَا يَمَسُّهُمْ فِيهَا نَصَبٌ وَمَا هُم مِّنْهَا بِمُخْرَجِينَ (٤۸)
Therein neither weariness shall touch them, nor will they [ever] be expelled from it. (48)

Key Points

The word ghill (غلّ) means secret penetration. Therefore, bad characteristics take root in humans mysteriously and secretly. Sarīr (سریر) comes from the word surūr (joy/delight) and means a throne or couch. It is understood that reclining on a throne causes delight (surūr).

In these verses, Allah mentions eight heavenly rewards for the people of piety (taqwa): gardens, springs, health, security, removal of resentment, brotherhood, couches facing one another, freedom from all suffering, and eternity. In the previous verse, we read that except for the chosen ones of Allah, all people are subjected to the temptations of Iblis. In these verses, He states that even if a person does not reach the stage of the sincere (mukhlaṣīn) and the chosen ones, but steps onto the stage of piety (taqwa), he will still be covered by various divine blessings.

What is important is the combination of all these blessings in one place. For in this world, sometimes there are gardens but no springs; sometimes there are springs but no gardens; sometimes there are both, but no health; sometimes there are all three, but no security; and sometimes everything is present, but there is no purity and sincerity; and sometimes it is accompanied by various hardships and sufferings. And sometimes, when all types of blessings are gathered in one place, one must leave everything and depart. However, in the Hereafter, all types of material, spiritual, social, and psychological blessings are combined with eternity.

Messages

1- Glad tidings, alongside the preceding verses of warning, are an educational necessity. «لها سبعة ابواب ... انّ المتّقین...»

2- Abstaining from sin during these few days of worldly life brings eternal success. «انّ المتّقین ....و ما منها بمخرجین»

3- Heavenly blessings are diverse and numerous. «جنات و عیون»

4- Heavenly blessings are flawless. Gardens come with springs, health with security, brotherhood with sincerity, and comfort with permanence.

5- The blessings of Paradise are comprehensive. They are material, like gardens and springs; spiritual, like security and purity; social, like fraternity and brotherhood; and above all, they are accompanied by divine pleasure and congratulations. «ادخلوها بسلام»

6- Malice is incompatible with brotherhood. First, resentments must be removed, and then brotherhood is realized. «و نزعنا ... اخوانا»

7- Anger, dissatisfaction, and intolerance toward one another are not always signs that one party is right and the other is wrong. «ان المتقین... نزعنا ما فى صدورهم من غل» (Because sometimes two people, even though they are inhabitants of Paradise and share a sacred goal, view an issue from different angles and consider the other's action invalid, becoming angry with him. In the Hereafter, all resentments will be removed by the Will of Allah.)

8- To remove resentment, one must seek help from Allah. «نزعنا», «ربّنا لاتجعل فى قلوبنا غلاّ للذین امنوا»

9- Bad characteristics, like shackles (ghill) and chains, enslave man. «من غلٍّ»

10- The inhabitants of Paradise have no issue regarding the differences in their ranks. «و نزعنا ما فى صدورهم من غلّ»

Ayahs

۞ نَبِّئْ عِبَادِىٓ أَنِّىٓ أَنَا ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٤۹)
Inform my servants that I am indeed the All-forgiving, the All-merciful, (49)
وَأَنَّ عَذَابِى هُوَ ٱلْعَذَابُ ٱلْأَلِيمُ (٥٠)
and that My punishment is a painful punishment. (50)

Key Points

The Quran divides people in these verses into four categories: 1. The sincere (Mukhlasin) (prophets and saints) over whom Iblis has no dominion. 2. The pious (Muttaqin) who are the inhabitants of Paradise. 3. Sinners who repent, whose pardon the Prophet announces in this verse. 4. Rebellious sinners who are warned of the painful punishment.

Messages

  1. Divine mercy is the greatest and best message. «نبّى ء» (Naba' refers to an important piece of news.)

  2. Sinners are also servants of Allah. «عبادى»

  3. Do not allow doubt or uncertainty regarding His grace. «انّى انا»

  4. His forgiveness is accompanied by grace and kindness; He not only forgives but also bestows grace. «الغفور الرحیم»

  5. Divine forgiveness and grace are profound and continuous. «غفور رحیم» (The form of Ghafur and Rahim indicates depth and repetition.)

  6. Divine grace and mercy precede His wrath. «اَنَا الغفور الرحیم و انّ عذابى»

  7. Let us not become arrogant due to Divine mercy, because His punishment is painful. «الغفور الرحیم - العذاب الالیم»

Ayahs

وَنَبِّئْهُمْ عَن ضَيْفِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ (٥۱)
And inform them about the guests of Abraham, (51)
إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمًا قَالَ إِنَّا مِنكُمْ وَجِلُونَ (٥۲)
when they entered into his presence and said, ‘Peace!’ He said, ‘We are indeed afraid of you.’ (52)
قَالُوا۟ لَا تَوْجَلْ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَـٰمٍ عَلِيمٍ (٥۳)
They said, ‘Do not be afraid. Indeed we give you the good news of a wise son.’ (53)
قَالَ أَبَشَّرْتُمُونِى عَلَىٰٓ أَن مَّسَّنِىَ ٱلْكِبَرُ فَبِمَ تُبَشِّرُونَ (٥٤)
He said, ‘Do you give me good news though old age has befallen me? What is the good news that you bring me?’ (54)
قَالُوا۟ بَشَّرْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ فَلَا تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْقَـٰنِطِينَ (٥٥)
They said, ‘We bring you good news in truth; so do not be despondent.’ (55)
قَالَ وَمَن يَقْنَطُ مِن رَّحْمَةِ رَبِّهِۦٓ إِلَّا ٱلضَّآلُّونَ (٥٦)
He said, ‘Who despairs of his Lord’s mercy except the astray?!’ (56)

Key Points

One example of Allah's Mercy and Wrath is the story of the guests of Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام), where the angels, on the one hand, gave him the glad tidings of a son, and on the other hand, the news of the destruction of the people of Lot. The word ḍayf (ضیف) refers both to a single guest and to multiple guests. Allah once gave Prophet Abraham a son named Ishmael through his bondwoman (Hagar), and this specific glad tiding concerns his wife Sarah, through whom Allah gave him Isaac. Regarding Ishmael, He said: «بغلام حلیم»(21) (with a forbearing boy), and regarding Isaac, He said: «بغلام علیم» (with a knowing boy).

21) Surah As-Saffat, 101.

Messages

1- We should learn lessons from the bitter and sweet events of history. «نبّئهم» (The best histories are the lives of the Prophets, and the best historians are those great figures themselves.)

2- Sometimes, by Allah's Will, angels appear in human form and interact with people. «ضیف ابراهیم»

3- Greeting (saying salām) has been a divine etiquette throughout history. «فقالوا سلاما»

4- The knowledge and recognition of the Prophets are limited and conditional upon Allah's permission. «انا منكم وجلون» (Prophet Abraham did not recognize them initially and became fearful.)

5- We should convey bitter news alongside sweet news. «بشرنا» (According to Imam Baqir (عليه السلام), the sweetness of the news of the son was to compensate for the bitterness of the news of the destruction of the people of Lot.)(22)

6- Expressing astonishment at Allah's Power does not contradict Monotheism (Tawhid). «فبم تبشرون»

(Here, the astonishment of Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) is mentioned, and in Surah Hud, the astonishment of his wife Sarah is mentioned: «ءَاَلِدُ و اَنَا عجوز وهذا بعلى شیخاً»)(23)

7- Prophets are trained by divine etiquette. «لاتكن من القانطین»

8- The infallibility (‘ismah) of the Prophets does not contradict divine warnings. «لاتكن من القانطین»

9- Allah's prohibition is not always a sign that a wrong action has been committed; sometimes, prohibitions are preventative. «لاتكن من القانطین»

10- We should cure despair and hopelessness by remembering Allah's Mercy and Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «رحمة ربّه»

11- Those who trust in Allah's Knowledge, Power, and Mercy never despair. «من یقنط ...الا الضّالون»(24)

22) Tafsir Safi.

23) Surah Hud, 72.

24) One of the manifestations of the ḍāllūn (those astray) whom we disavow in prayer are the despairing and hopeless individuals.

Ayahs

قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (٥۷)
He said, ‘O messengers, what is now your errand?’ (57)
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ (٥۸)
They said, ‘We have been sent toward a guilty people, (58)

Messages

1- Angels would inform the Divine leaders before carrying out a mission. «قالوا انّا ارسلنا»

2- Angels are the agents of Allah. Just as they pray for the believers «و یستغفرون للّذین آمنوا»(25), they also become the bearers of Divine wrath upon the criminals. «ارسلنا»

3- Hierarchy must be maintained. Although the matter concerned the people of Lot, the angels first informed Prophet Abraham, and then Prophet Lot, because Prophet Lot was under the guardianship of Prophet Abraham. «انّا ارسلنا»

4- The punishment for criminals is not restricted only to the Hereafter. «ارسلنا، مجرمین»

5- If sin becomes widespread and there is no repentance, Divine wrath is certain. «ارسلنا الى قوم مجرمین»25) Ghāfir, 7.

Ayahs

إِلَّآ ءَالَ لُوطٍ إِنَّا لَمُنَجُّوهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٥۹)
[who shall perish] except the family of Lot. We will indeed deliver all of them, (59)
إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ قَدَّرْنَآ ۙ إِنَّهَا لَمِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (٦٠)
except his wife, [who], We have ordained, will indeed be among those who remain behind.’ (60)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ءَالَ لُوطٍ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (٦۱)
So when the messengers came to Lot’s family, (61)
قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ (٦۲)
he said, ‘You are indeed strangers [to me].’ (62)

Key Points

Ghabirīn (غابرین) is the plural of ghābir (غابر), meaning those who remain behind (the laggards). The remaining dust is called ghubār (غبار). Prophet Abraham and Prophet Lot did not recognize the angels upon their first encounter. Thus, Abraham said: «انّا منكم وجلون» (Indeed, we are fearful of you), and Lot said: «قوم منكرون» (You are unfamiliar people). Of course, these angels visited Prophet Lot in the guise of handsome and beautiful young men, and the Prophet became worried about the presence of such guests due to the corruption of his society.

Messages

1- The true followers of the prophets are also safeguarded from worldly punishments. «الاّ ال لوط»

2- The opposition of Lot's wife to him is the best proof of human free will and that man is not condemned or compelled by his environment. «الاّ امراته»

3- Economic dependence does not necessitate intellectual or cultural dependence. (Lot's wife ate Lot's bread, but she thought against him).

4- In the school of the prophets, the principle is the rule (law), not the relationship. Therefore, even the wife of a prophet is afflicted by Divine wrath. «الاّ امراته انها لمن الغابرین»

(Family ties must not prevent the execution of Divine objectives).

5- There have been failures even in the marriages of the prophets. «الاّ امراته»

6- The account of the men of God is separate from the account of their unworthy relatives and associates. «الاّ امراته»

7- Divine wrath and punishment are according to calculation and plan. «قدّرنا»

8- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah in punishing the wicked recognizes no distinction between insider or outsider, woman or man, famous or unknown. «انّها لمن الغابرین»

9- Prophets do not possess knowledge of the unseen (ilm al-ghayb) inherently. (Prophet Lot did not recognize the angels). «قوم منكرون»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ بَلْ جِئْنَـٰكَ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ (٦۳)
They said, ‘Indeed, we bring you what they used to doubt. (63)
وَأَتَيْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَإِنَّا لَصَـٰدِقُونَ (٦٤)
We bring you the truth, and indeed, we speak truly. (64)
فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱتَّبِعْ أَدْبَـٰرَهُمْ وَلَا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنكُمْ أَحَدٌ وَٱمْضُوا۟ حَيْثُ تُؤْمَرُونَ (٦٥)
Take your family in a watch of the night; and follow in their rear, and none of you should turn round, and proceed as you are bidden.’ (65)
وَقَضَيْنَآ إِلَيْهِ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْأَمْرَ أَنَّ دَابِرَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ مَقْطُوعٌ مُّصْبِحِينَ (٦٦)
We apprised him of the matter that these will be rooted out by dawn. (66)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly mentioned that the disbelievers requested the prophets to hasten Allah's wrath and punishment, saying: «فأتنا بما تعدنا ان كنت من الصّادقین»(26). They took all warnings as a joke and doubted Allah's wrath, whether in this world or the Hereafter. Allah states in these verses that the wrath doubted by the disbelievers will certainly come. «قِطع» is the plural of «قطعه» (qit'ah), and it refers to the major part of the night that has passed.

26) A'raf, 70.

Messages

1- Do not take Allah's warnings and threats as a joke. «جئناك»

2- Divine punishments are based on justice, truth, and the deserving nature of the criminals. «بالحق»

3- Utilize the heedlessness of the disbelievers for the salvation of the believers. «فاسر باهلك بِقطع من الّیل»

4- The movement of the prophets is under the supervision and command of Allah. «تؤمرون»

5- In important events, Allah's grace provides moment-by-moment aid. (When to migrate: «بقطع من الّیل»; with whom: «باهلك»; by what method: «لایلتفت منكم احد»; and to what destination: «حیث تؤمرون»)

6- Allah informs the prophets before the destruction of the disbelievers. «و قضینا الیه»

7- Allah is capable of destroying a generation or a group in an instant. «دابر»

Ayahs

وَجَآءَ أَهْلُ ٱلْمَدِينَةِ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ (٦۷)
The people of the city came, rejoicing. (67)
قَالَ إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ضَيْفِى فَلَا تَفْضَحُونِ (٦۸)
He said, ‘These are indeed my guests. Do not bring dishonour on me. (68)
وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخْزُونِ (٦۹)
Be wary of Allah and do not humiliate me.’ (69)
قَالُوٓا۟ أَوَلَمْ نَنْهَكَ عَنِ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۷٠)
They said, ‘Did we not forbid you from [defending ] strangers?’ (70)

Key Points

It is mentioned in narrations that Lot's wife informed the sinners that handsome guests had come for Prophet Lot (عليه السلام), and they, pursuing their wicked objective, rushed toward Lot's house. But Allah destroyed them before they could take any action. (27) It is understood from the Ayah that even a sinner can see an angel.

27) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

1- In forbidding evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar), use compassionate and humane words. «ضیفی»

2- Honor (reputation) is highly valuable. «فلا تفضحون، و لاتخزون»

3- Insulting the guest is an insult to the host. «فلا تفضحون»

4- In a corrupt society, the criminal and the sinner become the demanding party (or the accuser). «أو لم ننهك»

5- In a corrupt society, the men of God do not have the right to dispose of their own possessions (or manage their own affairs). «أو لم ننهك...»

Ayah

قَالَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ بَنَاتِىٓ إِن كُنتُمْ فَـٰعِلِينَ (۷۱)
He said, ‘These are my daughters, [marry them] if you should do anything.’ (71)

Key Points

Question: Could Lot (عليه السلام) have given his daughters to the disbelievers, which is why He proposed this? Answer: It is possible that the intention was marriage accompanied by their conversion to faith, or that at that time, marriage with disbelievers was not prohibited. واللَّه العالم (And Allah knows best).

Messages

1- To deter people from sin, first offer legal and permissible ways (to fulfill desires). «هؤلاء بناتى»

2- In enjoining good, forbidding evil, and preventing sin, invest yourselves (make sacrifices). «بناتى»

3- The school of the Prophets is not about prohibiting pleasures and suppressing instincts, but rather guiding their control. «هؤلاء بناتى ان كنتم فاعلین»

Ayahs

لَعَمْرُكَ إِنَّهُمْ لَفِى سَكْرَتِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (۷۲)
By your life, they were bewildered in their drunkenness. (72)
فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ مُشْرِقِينَ (۷۳)
So the Cry seized them at sunrise, (73)
فَجَعَلْنَا عَـٰلِيَهَا سَافِلَهَا وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً مِّن سِجِّيلٍ (۷٤)
and We made its topmost part its nethermost, and rained on them stones of shale. (74)

Key Points

‘Umar (عُمر) and ‘Amr (عَمر) both have the same meaning, but when taking an oath, the word ‘Amr (عَمر) is used. (28) In the Qur’an, an oath has not been sworn by the life of any Prophet other than the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. (Of course, Maraghi says: the intent of “la‘amruka” (لَعَمْرُكَ) is the oath of the angels by the life of Prophet Lot.) “Ya‘mahūn” (يَعْمَهُونَ) is derived from ‘amah (عَمَه), meaning bewilderment, and “mushriqīn” (مُشْرِقِينَ) means entering into the illumination and brightness of the morning. Perhaps the stoning was intended to destroy those who had survived when the city was overturned, or it was for the complete obliteration and destruction of the city.

28) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Swearing by the name of the Prophet is permissible. “la‘amruka” (لَعَمْرُكَ)

2- Sin and deviation erase human intellect and awareness, “sakratihim” (سَكْرَتِهِمْ), and prevent him from recognition. “ya‘mahūn” (يَعْمَهُونَ)

3- Those intoxicated by the world must be awakened by heavenly cries. “sakratihim” (سَكْرَتِهِمْ), “aṣ-ṣayḥah” (الصَّيْحَةُ)

4- Sound and a cry can cause a city to be overturned. “fa-akhadhat-humu aṣ-ṣayḥatu... fa-ja‘alnā” (فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ... فَجَعَلْنَا)

5- The Hand of Allah is open; just as He sends down the rain of mercy from the sky, He can send down a rain of stones and punishment. “amṭarnā ‘alayhim ḥijāratan” (أَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً)

6- Three types of punishment: the cry, the overturning of the city, and stoning, are the retribution decreed for the sin of sodomy (liwaṭ).

Ayahs

إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّلْمُتَوَسِّمِينَ (۷٥)
There are indeed signs in that for the percipient. (75)
وَإِنَّهَا لَبِسَبِيلٍ مُّقِيمٍ (۷٦)
Indeed it is on a standing road, (76)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۷۷)
and there is indeed a sign in that for the faithful. (77)

Key Points

The term mutawassim (متوسّم), derived from wasm (وسم) meaning 'to leave an impression' or 'to mark,' refers to someone who can deduce realities from the slightest trace, meaning they possess insight (firasah) and intelligence. It is mentioned in narrations that the intended meaning of al-mutawassimin (متوسمین) is the Prophet and his Ahl al-Bayt (Family).(29) A person said to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: "I have a matter [to ask about]." The Imam said: "Do you want Me to tell you what your question is before you ask it?" That person asked in astonishment: "How do You know what is in my mind?" The Imam replied: "By al-tawassum (بالتَّوسّم)," and then recited this Ayah.(30) It is possible that the meaning of the Ayah, إِنَّهَا لَبِسَبِيلٍ مُّقِيمٍ (Innahā labisabīlin muqīm), is that Allah's wrath is not exclusive to the people of Lot, but rather it is a fixed path and tradition for all criminals throughout history.(31)

29) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 30) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 31) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Historical events are signs (آيَاتٍ) for intelligent believers, and a single sign (آيَةً) for ordinary believers. آيَاتٍ , آيَةً

2- Preserving the relics and remains of past nations serves as a lesson for future generations. إِنَّهَا لَبِسَبِيلٍ مُّقِيمٍ

Ayahs

وَإِن كَانَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْأَيْكَةِ لَظَـٰلِمِينَ (۷۸)
Indeed the inhabitants of Aykah were [also] wrongdoers. (78)
فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ وَإِنَّهُمَا لَبِإِمَامٍ مُّبِينٍ (۷۹)
So We took vengeance on them, and indeed the two of them are on an open highway. (79)

Key Points

"Al-Aykah" (الأيكة) means a thicket or intertwined trees. The "Companions of Al-Aykah" (أصحاب الأيكة) refer to the people of Prophet Shu'ayb, who lived in a pleasant, well-watered, and heavily wooded region between Hijaz and Sham (Syria). In a narration from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, it is stated that the Companions of Madyan and the Companions of Al-Aykah were two nations, and Shu'ayb was the Prophet to both of them. Both committed disbelief, but they were afflicted by two types of punishment: one by the punishment of "Yawm al-Ẓullah" (the Day of the Shade) and the other by the punishment of "Ṣayḥah" (the Cry). (32) Since we have no claim upon Allah, whatever we receive is His "In'ām" (favor/bestowal). However, regarding divine retribution, because His wrath is due to our own sins, faults, and deserving actions, the word "Intiqām" (vengeance/retribution) is used.

32) Tafsir Furqan from Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 4, p. 103.

Messages

1- Let the oppressors know that their wickedness and tyranny will be recounted for others throughout history. «و ان كان اصحاب الایكة لظالمین»

2- When designing roads, we should try to ensure that the sites of historical events are visible to passersby. «لبامام مبین»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْحِجْرِ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۸٠)
Certainly the inhabitants of Hijr denied the apostles. (80)
وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا فَكَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ (۸۱)
We had given them Our signs but they disregarded them. (81)
وَكَانُوا۟ يَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا ءَامِنِينَ (۸۲)
They used to hew out dwellings from mountains feeling secure. (82)
فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ مُصْبِحِينَ (۸۳)
So the Cry seized them at dawn, (83)
فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۸٤)
and what they used to earn did not avail them. (84)

Key Points

"Al-Hijr" (الحِجر) is the name of a city where the people of Thamūd lived, and generally, it is also used to refer to a place of residence. "Hijr" (حِجر) means a mother's lap, and "Hijr Isma'il" and "Hujrah" (room) are derived from this same word (33). The name of this Surah is taken from this Ayah. The word 'mursalin' (messengers) suggests that the people of Al-Hijr had other prophets besides Prophet Salih (عليه السلام). Some say that since the denial of one prophet is essentially the denial of all prophets, the plural 'mursalin' is used. The intent of 'sayhah' (cry/blast) here is a thunderbolt, based on the Ayah: «انذرتكم صاعقة مثل صاعقة عاد و ثمود» (34).

33) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 34) Fussilat, 13.

Messages

1- All prophets possessed miracles. «آتیناهم آیاتنا»

2- Obstinate individuals do not allow themselves the opportunity to think. «آتیناهم ... فكانوا...»

3- Turning away from the truth and ignoring the men of truth is a habit for some groups. «فكانوا عنها معرضین»

4- Do not be arrogant about strong, stone houses, for they disintegrate before the Divine Will. «ینحتون من الجبال بیوتا ... فاخذتهم الصیحة»

Ayahs

وَمَا خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ لَـَٔاتِيَةٌ ۖ فَٱصْفَحِ ٱلصَّفْحَ ٱلْجَمِيلَ (۸٥)
We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them except with consummate wisdom, and indeed the Hour is bound to come. So forbear with a graceful forbearance. (85)
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ ٱلْخَلَّـٰقُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۸٦)
Indeed your Lord is the All-creator, the All-knowing. (86)

Key Points

The word Safh (overlooking/forgiveness) is derived from Safhah, meaning "face." The phrase Fasfah as-Safh means "turn away," but not out of wrath, rather out of pardon and overlooking. Similarly, Imam Reza (عليه السلام) interpreted Safh Jamil (beautiful overlooking) as forgiveness without subsequent accountability or reproach. (35)

35) Tafsir Nemooneh.

Messages

1- Creation is purposeful, and its purpose is truth (Haqq). «ما خلقنا... الاّ بالحقّ»

2- Creation is purposeful, and its path leads to the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «انّ الساعة لاتیة»

3- If you do not have the power to punish the disbelievers, do not worry; there is a Day of Resurrection coming. «انّ الساعة لاتیة فاصفح»

4- The foundation of moral issues, overlooking, and forgiveness must be faith in the Origin (Mabda'), the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and correct goal-setting. «خلقنا... بالحقّ وان الساعة لآتیة فاصفح»

5- Now that the Resurrection is true and everyone's account will be settled, do not worry about the schemes and obstinacy of the disbelievers, and forgive them. «انّ الساعة لاتیة فاصفح»

6- The One who commands overlooking is Allah, Who is the Educator, the Creator, and the All-Knowing. «انّ ربّك هو الخلاق العلیم» (He knows what effect pardon and overlooking have on the spirit of the individual and society, and on attracting and developing people; therefore, acting upon the command of Safh should not be difficult for you.)

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكَ سَبْعًا مِّنَ ٱلْمَثَانِى وَٱلْقُرْءَانَ ٱلْعَظِيمَ (۸۷)
Certainly We have given you [the surah of] the seven oft-repeated verses and the great Quran. (87)

Key Points

It is mentioned in both Shi'a and Sunni narrations that the intent of "سبعاً من المثانى" (seven of the oft-repeated verses) is Surah Al-Hamd (Al-Fatiha). This is because this Surah is recited twice in every prayer, and it is mentioned in a narration that Allah says: I have divided Surah Al-Hamd into two parts between Myself and My servant; half of it concerns Me, and half of it relates to My servants. (From "بسم اللَّه" up to "مالك یوم الدین" relates to Allah, and from "ایّاك نَعبد," which is the declaration of servitude, seeking help, and supplication, relates to the servants.) Of course, some have said that the intent of "مثانى" (Mathani) is the Qur'an itself, and "سبعاً من المثانى" means seven verses from the Qur'an, which is Surah Al-Hamd. The proof for this statement is the Ayah: «اللَّه نزل احسن الحدیث كتاباً متشابهاً مثانى»(36) meaning, Allah sent down the best statement, a Book whose verses are similar, harmonious, and oft-repeated. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «من اعطاه اللَّه القرآن فَراى اَنّ رجلاً اعطى افضل مما اعطى فقد صغّر عظیماً و عظّم صغیراً»(37) meaning, whoever is given the Qur'an by Allah and then imagines that someone else has been given something better than what he was given, has belittled a great thing (the Qur'an) and magnified a small thing (that which is not the Qur'an).

36) Zumar, 23. 37) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Legislation is the right of the Creator, and divine law (Tashrī') must be based on creation (Takwīn). «خلقنا السموات و الارض... اتیناك... القرآن العظیم»

2- Repetition is a principle in educational matters. "مثانى" (The repetition of the revelation of verses, the repetition of words, the repetition of stories, the repetition of Divine attributes, the repetition of verses about punishment and the Resurrection, the repetition of the destiny of nations, the repetition of Divine favors, the repetition of commands, and the repetition of the recitation of the Qur'an).

3- Surah Al-Hamd is equivalent to the Qur'an, even though the number of its verses is seven. «سبعاً من المثانى و القرآن العظیم»

Ayah

لَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعْنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجًا مِّنْهُمْ وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَٱخْفِضْ جَنَاحَكَ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۸۸)
Do not extend your glance toward what We have provided to certain groups of them, and do not grieve for them, and lower your wing to the faithful, (88)

Key Points

Prohibition (Nahy) is not always issued after the commission of an act; rather, sometimes it is for warning and prevention. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله never desired the worldly possessions of the disbelievers. Therefore, Allah’s prohibition serves as a warning so that other believers do not fix their gaze upon the world of the disbelievers. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever stares at what is in the hands of others, his worries and grief increase, and his distress will not be cured.(38) One of the emphatic recommendations of the Quran to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the believers is the recommendation for gentleness, mercy, and patience towards the people of faith. Including: «واصبر نفسك مع الّذین یدعون ربّهم»(39) (Be patient with those who constantly call upon their Lord), «اذلة على المؤمنین»(40) (They are humble towards the believers), «رحماء بینهم»(41) (They are merciful among themselves). Azwāj (pairs/kinds) includes both men and women, and it is possible that the intention here is the various classes of people and the disbelievers.(42)

38) Tafsir Safi. 39) Kahf, 28. 40) Ma’idah, 54. 41) Fath, 29. 42) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- In the face of the resources of others, rely on your own capabilities and resources. Since you possess the Quran, «اتیناك سبعاً» (We have given you seven [oft-repeated verses]), do not attach your heart to the world of the disbelievers, «و لا تَمُدّن...»

2- Do not stare at or become attached to worldly blessings. «لا تَمُدّنّ عینیك» (Because the world is both little «قلیل», and fleeting «عرض», and it is a bud that never fully opens for anyone «زهرة الحیاة الدّنیا»).

3- Staring causes interest and attachment. (Do not look, lest the heart becomes captive) «لاتمدّنّ عینیك» (Sin and deviation must be prevented at the source).

A cry from both the eye and the heart For whatever the eye sees, the heart remembers.

4- Only he who is not entangled in materialism can guide people toward spirituality «لاتمدنّ عینیك» (The address of the Ayah is to the Prophet).

5- Not everyone who pursues the world attains it. «ازواجاً منهم»

6- One should not grieve for those who are not amenable to guidance. «لاتحزن»

7- Humility and gentleness towards the world and world-worshippers? Never. «لاتمدن» But towards the religious, always. «واخفض جناحك للمؤمنین»

8- The leader and educator must show gentleness and affection towards the people. «واخفض»

Ayah

وَقُلْ إِنِّىٓ أَنَا ٱلنَّذِيرُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۸۹)
and say, ‘I am indeed a manifest warner [of punishment from God],’ (89)

Messages

1- Just as softness and affection are necessary toward the believers and friends, «واخفض جناحك للمؤمنین» (And lower your wing to the believers), firmness against opponents is also essential. «قل انّى انا النذیر» (Say, 'Indeed, I am the Warner.')

2- In some instances, warnings must be direct (without intermediary). «انّى انا»

3- In the face of disbelief (kufr), threat and warning are necessary. «انا النذیر»

4- The warning must be explicit and serious. «النذیر المبین»

Ayahs

كَمَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَى ٱلْمُقْتَسِمِينَ (۹٠)
like that We sent down upon those who split into bands, (90)
ٱلَّذِينَ جَعَلُوا۟ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ عِضِينَ (۹۱)
who represented the Quran as magic. (91)

Key Points

In the exegeses, three meanings have been stated for al-Muqtasimīn (المقتسمین): 1. They are the leaders of the disbelievers who divided forces during the Hajj season along the roads and entrances to Mecca, telling travelers that a person named Muhammad makes claims, warning them not to listen to his words, and describing the Prophet as a soothsayer, sorcerer, and madman. 2. A group that divided the Quran among themselves so that each one could bring something similar to a part of the Quran. 3. Those who acted upon a part of the Quran and abandoned another part. The word 'iḍīn (عضین), which is the plural of 'iḍah (عضه), means dispute and contention, or it is derived from 'uḍw (عضو), meaning to cut into pieces.

Messages

  1. Let us learn lessons from history. «كما انزلنا»

  2. The Divine tradition regarding the punishment for sins is fixed and enduring. «كما انزلنا»

  3. The punishment for those who fragment the heavenly Books is Divine wrath. «المقتسمین»

  4. The distortion and fragmentation of heavenly Books by the unworthy is not new. «المقتسمین»

(A believer is one who accepts the entire Quran and says, «كلُّ من عند ربّنا» (43), meaning that everything revealed is from the Lord, and does not ignore or deny some parts.)

(43) Aal-i-Imran, 7.

Ayahs

فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَسْـَٔلَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۹۲)
By your Lord, We will question them all (92)
عَمَّا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۹۳)
concerning what they used to do. (93)

Messages

1- The worldly punishment of the disbelievers does not remove their punishment in the Hereafter. «انزلنا على المقتسمین ... فو ربك لنسئلنّهم»

2- The prerequisite for nurturing (or guidance) is questioning and reprimand. «فو ربّك لنسئلنّهم»

3- Paying attention to the questioning and accountability on the Day of Resurrection, and the fact that everyone will be questioned, «اجمعین», and about all deeds, «عما كانوا یعملون», is one of the factors leading to awakening.

4- The duty of the Prophet is to warn, but the accountability of the people rests with Allah. «انا النذیر لنسئلنّهم...»

Ayahs

فَٱصْدَعْ بِمَا تُؤْمَرُ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۹٤)
So proclaim what you have been commanded, and turn away from the polytheists. (94)
إِنَّا كَفَيْنَـٰكَ ٱلْمُسْتَهْزِءِينَ (۹٥)
Indeed We will suffice you against the deriders (95)
ٱلَّذِينَ يَجْعَلُونَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ ۚ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (۹٦)
—those who set up another deity besides Allah. Soon they will know! (96)

Key Points

Sad‘ (صدع) means to split open and to make manifest (or reveal). The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله spent 3 years secretly propagating the message to the people. When the verse, «فاصدع بما تؤمر» was revealed, He made His call public and told the people: If you accept My invitation, the governance and honor of this world and the Hereafter will be yours. However, they mocked the Prophet and they came to Abu Talib, the Prophet’s uncle, complaining that Muhammad was deceiving their youth, saying that if He desired money, a wife, or status, they would give it to Him. The Prophet told His uncle: Dear uncle, My word is a Divine message, and I will never give it up! The disbelievers requested Abu Talib to hand Muhammad over to them, but he did not accept their request.(44)

44) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- Propagation has stages; sometimes it is public, and sometimes it is secret, requiring taqiyyah (dissimulation). «فاصدع»

2- The propagator should not pay attention to the clamor of the opponents. «واعرض عن المشركین»

3- Whenever you give someone a difficult command or responsibility, you must support and secure him. «فاصدع , انا كفیناك»

4- Allah's support is certain. «كفیناك»

5- A truthful and logical word is never without opponents. «المستهزئین»

6- If they mock you, do not be worried or distressed, because they have not even respected the sanctity of Allah. «یجعلون مع اللَّه الهاً اخر»

7- Those who turn to other than Allah will realize their mistake. «فسوف یعلمون»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ (۹۷)
Certainly We know that you become upset because of what they say. (97)
فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَكُن مِّنَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ (۹۸)
So celebrate the praise of your Lord and be among those who prostrate, (98)
وَٱعْبُدْ رَبَّكَ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِيَكَ ٱلْيَقِينُ (۹۹)
and worship your Lord until certainty comes to you. (99)

Key Points

It is famous among commentators that the meaning of "certainty" (یقین) in these verses is death, which is the most certain matter for humanity. Just as it is mentioned in Ayah 46 of Surah Al-Muddaththir, spoken by the disbelievers: «و كنّا نكذب بیوم الدین حتّى اتانا الیقین» (And we used to deny the Day of Judgment until certainty came to us). We used to deny the Resurrection until the moment of death. Although the meaning of certainty (Yaqin) is not death, death is a context for achieving certainty, because when death arrives, all veils are lifted, and man attains certainty regarding realities. Some deviants have used the verse «واعبد ربّك حتّى یاتیك الیقین» (And worship your Lord until certainty comes to you) as a pretext, saying: If you reach certainty, worship is no longer necessary! They are heedless of the fact that the address of the verse is to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, who had attained certainty from the very first day. In the face of the temptations and threats of the disbelievers, He would say: If you place the moon in one of my hands and the sun in the other, I will not abandon my duty. Yet, despite that certainty, He continued to worship until the last moment of His life.

Messages

  1. If we know that our suffering is under Allah's supervision, enduring difficulties becomes easy. «نعلم» (We know).
  2. The capacity of all individuals, even the Prophets, is limited. «یضیق صدرك» (Your breast is constrained).
  3. Sometimes obstinacy and nonsense put pressure even on the most broad-minded individuals. «یضیق صدرك» (Your breast is constrained), despite Allah having said: «الم نشرح لك صدرك» (Did We not expand for you your breast?).
  4. The Prophet's breast was not constrained during wars, but He was distressed by the words (of the disbelievers). «یضیق صدرك بمایقولون» (Your breast is constrained by what they say).
  5. The remedy for spiritual and psychological pressure is prayer (Salat), prostration (Sujud), glorification (Tasbih), and praise of Allah. «یضیق... فسبّح بحمد ربّك... واعبد» (Is constrained... then glorify with the praise of your Lord... and worship). (Ibn Abbas says: Whenever the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله became sad, He would stand up for prayer.)(45)
  6. More important than worship is its continuity. «واعبد ربّك حتّى یاتیك الیقین» (And worship your Lord until certainty comes to you).
  7. Persistence in servitude to the Truth (Allah) is the cause of attaining certainty. «واعبد ... یاتیك الیقین» (And worship... certainty comes to you).

(45) Tafsir Namuneh.
